items.title: Four new astronauts arrive at the International Space Station to replace NASA's evacuated crew
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The International Space Station returned to full strength with Saturday’s arrival of four new astronauts to replace colleagues who bailed early because of health concerns. SpaceX delivered the U.S., French and Russian astronauts a day after launching them from Cape Canaveral.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Celebrations at Brazil House as Lucas Pinheiro Braathen wins gold at Winter Olympics
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Brazilian ski racer Lucas Pinheiro Braathen turned in a powerful final run to win the Olympic giant slalom Saturday and earn South America’s first medal at a Winter Games.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Marco Rubio meets Zelenskyy at the Munich Security Conference
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy held a meeting Saturday with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference.

items.title: UK says Russia killed opposition leader Alexei Navalny using dart frog toxin
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny was poisoned by the Kremlin with a lethal toxin derived from the skin of poison dart frogs, five European countries said Saturday.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: From robot vacuums to helicopters: how people mimic Olympic curling
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Curling is a popular sport at the Winter Olympics. And people have found their own ways to mimic the sport from using robot vacuums to helicopters.

#winterolympics2026 #winterolympics #milancortina #curling</article>

items.title: Dust storm engulfs Gaza, Israel and other parts of the Middle East
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In the Gaza Strip, the dust blanketed areas where people are living in make-shift tents beside damaged buildings.

In Israel, the health ministry gave a statement warning that southwesterly winds carrying dust from North Africa were causing high levels of air pollution across the country.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Olympic mascots Milo and Tina take off in popularity
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Milan Cortina Olympic mascots, the eponymous Milo and Tina, have taken off in popularity. Plushies of the stoat siblings are flying off the shelves, and many of the official Olympics stores in the host cities are already sold out, less than a week into the Winter Games.</article>

items.title: Rubio tells the Munich Security Conference that the US and Europe 'belong together'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio offered a friendly and reassuring assessment of America’s relations with Europe under President Donald Trump’s administration, saying Saturday that the blunt tone of previous criticisms was intended to spur a renaissance in trans-Atlantic ties.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Christian McCaffrey, Jameis Winston and more NFL stars join On Football
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Christian McCaffrey, Jameis Winston, Hall of Famer Devin Hester and others join On Football with Rob Maaddi from San Francisco for a Super Bowl LX wrap-up edition.</article>

items.title: Merz calls on the US and Europe to 'repair' trans-Atlantic trust 'together'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has called on the U.S. and Europe to “repair and revive trans-Atlantic trust together.” He argued Friday that even the U.S. isn’t powerful enough to go it alone in an world whose old order has withered. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Sex workers at a Nevada brothel are fighting to unionize
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Nevada is the only state where people can legally purchase sex, and now sex workers at one of the state’s oldest brothels are fighting to become the nation’s first to be unionized. (AP Video: Thomas Peipert).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Mark Carney and opposition leader hold hands during Canada school shooting vigil
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and the country’s opposition leaders paid tribute to the victims of one of the worst mass shootings in the country’s history at a vigil in the devastated northern British Columbia town.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: 'The Simpsons' at 800 episodes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Eight hundred episodes, 37 seasons, and one four-fingered family that refuses to age. As “The Simpsons” hits a milestone few series have ever glimpsed this weekend, the architects behind Springfield are reflecting on the choices that turned crude 1987 shorts from “The Tracey Ullman Show” into a cultural juggernaut. The full AP mini-documentary: https://youtu.be/N8z81-rfS64
#thesimpsons #simpsons</article>
